metadata.summary: A new backend based on pyqtgraph for the 2D-Data-Browser in MNE-Python

documentation:
# mne-qt-browser

## A new backend based on pyqtgraph for the 2D-Data-Browser in MNE-Python.

![Screenshot of mne-qt-browser](https://github.com/mne-tools/mne-qt-browser/raw/main/screenshot.png)

This repository hosts the code for an alternative backend for plotting 2D-Data with 
[MNE-Python](https://github.com/mne-tools/mne-python).

The backend is based on [pyqtgraph](https://github.com/pyqtgraph/pyqtgraph) 
which uses Qt's [Graphics View Framework](https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/graphicsview.html)
for the plotting.
Development started as a [2021's Google Summer of Code Project](https://github.com/marsipu/gsoc2021).

## Installation
Install **full MNE-Python version 1.0 or greater** with the instructions provided [here](https://mne.tools/stable/install/mne_python.html#d-plotting-and-source-analysis) or install **minimal MNE-Python** with
### pip
```
pip install "mne>=1.0" matplotlib mne-qt-browser
```
or
### conda
```
conda install -c conda-forge mne-base matplotlib mne-qt-browser
```

## Update
Refer to the [MNE-Python documentation](https://mne.tools/stable/install/updating.html) for updating MNE-Python.
To update this package, do:
```
pip install -U mne-qt-browser
```
To update this package to the development version, do:
```
pip install -U --no-deps https://github.com/mne-tools/mne-qt-browser/archive/refs/heads/main.zip
```

## Usage

The backend supports plotting for the following MNE-Python methods:

- [`mne.io.Raw.plot()`](https://mne.tools/dev/generated/mne.io.Raw.html?highlight=raw%20plot#mne.io.Raw.plot)
- [`mne.Epochs.plot()`](https://mne.tools/dev/generated/mne.Epochs.html?highlight=epochs%20plot#mne.Epochs.plot)
- [`mne.preprocessing.ICA.plot_sources(raw)`](https://mne.tools/dev/generated/mne.preprocessing.ICA.html?highlight=ica%20plot_sources#mne.preprocessing.ICA.plot_sources)
- [`mne.preprocessing.ICA.plot_sources(epochs)`](https://mne.tools/dev/generated/mne.preprocessing.ICA.html?highlight=ica%20plot_sources#mne.preprocessing.ICA.plot_sources)

In the following example, we'll read M/EEG raw data from the MNE `sample` dataset
and plot it using the `qt`-backend.
(For mne-version >= 1.0 the `qt`-backend will be the default)

```python
from pathlib import Path
import mne

sample_dir = mne.datasets.sample.data_path()
raw_path = Path(sample_dir) / 'MEG' / 'sample' / 'sample_audvis_raw.fif'
raw = mne.io.read_raw(raw_path)

mne.viz.set_browser_backend('qt')  # Enable mne-qt-browser backend if mne < 1.0
raw.plot(block=True)
```

If the plot is not showing, search for solutions in the
[troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) section below.

This will use the `mne-qt-browser` for the current Python session. If you
want to make this change permanent, so you don't have to use the
`set_browser_backend()` each time after restarting Python, run the following
line to modify your MNE configuration file:

```python
import mne
mne.set_config('MNE_BROWSER_BACKEND', 'qt')
```

## Troubleshooting

### Running from a script

If you are running a script containing `raw.plot()` like

```console
python example_script.py
```

the plot will not stay open when the script is done. 

To solve this either change `raw.plot()` to `raw.plot(block=True)` or run the script with the interactive flag

```console
python -i example_script.py
```

### IPython

If the integration of the Qt event loop is not activated for IPython, a plot with `raw.plot()` will freeze.
Do avoid that either change `raw.plot()` to `raw.plot(block=True)` or activate the integration of the event loop with

```console
%gui qt5
```

### Report Bugs & Feature Requests

Please report bugs and feature requests in the [issues](https://github.com/mne-tools/mne-qt-browser/issues) of this repository.

### Development and testing

You can run a benchmark locally with:

```console
pytest -m benchmark mne_qt_browser
```

To run the PyQtGraph tests you have to run:
```
pytest mne_qt_browser/tests/test_pg_specific.py
```

Additionally, clone mne-python, and then run:

```console
pytest -m pgtest ../mne-python/mne/viz/tests
```

If you do not have OpenGL installed, this will currently raise errors, and
you'll need to add this line to `mne/conftest.py` after the `error::` line:

```raw
    ignore:.*PyOpenGL was not found.*:RuntimeWarning
```

functions.name: mne_qt_browser.tests.test_speed.test_scroll_speed_raw
documentation: Test the speed of a parameter.

functions.name: mne_qt_browser.tests.test_speed.test_scroll_speed_epochs_multicolor
documentation: Test scrolling speed in multicolor mode.

functions.name: mne_qt_browser.tests.test_pg_specific.test_pg_settings_dialog
documentation: Test Settings Dialog toggle on/off for pyqtgraph-backend.

functions.name: mne_qt_browser.tests.test_pg_specific.test_pg_help_dialog
documentation: Test Settings Dialog toggle on/off for pyqtgraph-backend.

functions.name: mne_qt_browser.tests.test_pg_specific.test_pg_toolbar_actions
documentation:
Test toolbar all actions combined.

Toolbar actions here create a separate QDialog window.
We test the state machine for each window toggle button.

functions.name: mne_qt_browser.tests.test_pg_specific.test_color_conversion
documentation: Test color conversions against manually run ones.
LAB values taken from colorspacious on 2024/06/10

modules.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure
documentation: Base classes and functions for 2D browser backends.

functions.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure._get_color
documentation: Wrap mkColor to accept all possible matplotlib color-specifiers.

functions.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure._methpartial
documentation: Use WeakMethod to create a partial method.

functions.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure._mousePress
documentation: mouse testing functions adapted from pyqtgraph

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.DataTrace.update_color
documentation: Update the color of the trace.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.DataTrace.update_range_idx
documentation: Update when view-range or ch_idx changes.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.DataTrace.update_ypos
documentation: Update when butterfly is toggled or ch_idx changes.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.DataTrace.set_ch_idx
documentation: Set the channel index and all deriving indices.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.DataTrace.update_data
documentation: Update data (fetch data from self.mne according to self.ch_idx).

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.TimeAxis.tickValues
documentation: Customize creation of axis values from visible axis range.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.ChannelAxis.tickValues
documentation: Customize creation of axis values from visible axis range.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.BaseScrollBar

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.BaseScrollBar.mousePressEvent
documentation:
Customize mouse click events.

Taken from: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/29710327/
how-to-override-qscrollbar-onclick-default-behaviour
Base Class for scrolling directly to the clicked position.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.OverviewBar

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.OverviewBar.set_background
documentation: Set the background-image for the selected overview-mode.

Provides overview over channels and current visible range.

Has different modes:
- channels: Display channel-types
- zscore: Display channel-wise zscore across time

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.RawViewBox
documentation: PyQtGraph-Wrapper for interaction with the View.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.VLineLabel

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.VLineLabel.valueChanged
documentation: Customize what happens on value change.
Label of the vline displaying the time.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.VLine
documentation: Marker to be placed inside the Trace-Plot.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.Crosshair

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.Crosshair.set_data
documentation: Set x and y data for crosshair point.
Continously updating marker inside the Trace-Plot.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.AnnotRegion._update_channel_annot_fills
documentation:
Update the FillBetweenItems for channel specific annotations.

FillBetweenItems are used to highlight channels associated with an annotation.
Start and stop are time in seconds.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.AnnotRegion.update_color
documentation:
Update color of annotation-region.

Parameters
----------
all_channels : bool
    all_channels should be False for channel specific annotations.
    These annotations will be more transparent with a dashed outline.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.AnnotRegion.update_label_pos
documentation: Update position of description-label from annotation-region.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.AnnotationDock._edit_description_all
documentation: Update descriptions of all annotations with the same description.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.AnnotationDock._edit_description_selected
documentation: Update description only of selected region.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.BrowserView
documentation: Customized View as part of GraphicsView-Framework.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.LoadThread

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.LoadThread.run
documentation: Load and process data in a separate QThread.
A worker object for precomputing in a separate QThread.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure._PGMetaClass
documentation:
Class is necessary to prevent a metaclass conflict.

The conflict arises due to the different types of QMainWindow and
BrowserBase.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.MNEQtBrowser._add_scalebars
documentation:
Add scalebars for all channel-types.

(scene handles showing them in when in view
range)

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.MNEQtBrowser.scale_all
documentation: Scale all traces by multiplying with step.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.MNEQtBrowser.change_nchan
documentation: Change number of channels by step.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.MNEQtBrowser._mouse_moved
documentation: Show Crosshair if enabled at mouse move.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.MNEQtBrowser._apply_downsampling
documentation:
Get ds-factor and apply ds with one of multiple methods.

The methods are taken from PlotDataItem in pyqtgraph
and adjusted to multi-channel data.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.MNEQtBrowser._close_event
documentation: Force calling of the MPL figure close event.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.MNEQtBrowser._check_close
documentation: Close annotations-mode before closing the browser.

methods.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.MNEQtBrowser._fake_click_on_toolbar_action
documentation: Trigger event associated with action 'action_name' in toolbar.

classes.name: mne_qt_browser._pg_figure.SignalBlocker
documentation: Wrapper to use QSignalBlocker as a context manager in PySide2.
